creator: stas@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi, Grzegorz.

Thank you for the patches!
I'll take a look at them soon.

However, as I stated in my previous emails, I don't want ecore and evas
ports to be combined into one.  They were splitted some time ago for a
reason: when all the functionality was in the single ports it was impossible
to depend on different features on ecore/evas independently -- you either have
to disable options in these ports at all and force the user to build them with
all options enabled (which renders them unusable in most cases - many users don't
want/have dbus/gnome/X11 stuff installed, think of embedded applications),
or you will end up with a lot of problems reports when a user disabled something
in ecore build, and now he can't build e17/whatever.  Been there, done that.
WRT taking much space - it's not true, each component contains only code related
to its function, and generic ecore/evas libraries were modified to support dynamic
loading of them.

I also somewhat opposed to dropping some of them obsoleted ports.  While most of
them has not useful functionality outside e17 project, some of them used by
3rd party applications and end users, like epeg (which is a very fast and small
jpeg thumbnailer, I believe nothing can be close in speed to it).  Dropping etk/ewl
makes a huge number of ports broken - we should address this also.

-- 
Stanislav Sedov
ST4096-RIPE
